The suggested dosage range is divided from one to three capsules taken with food throughout the day. IOP is the scientific acronym for intraocular pressure. It is traditionally measured in millimeters of mercury, abbreviated mmHg. The standard pressure range is 10 to 20 or 21 mmHg, with 15 being the norm. Pressures consistently above 21 mmHg may be termed “ocular hypertension” and warrant closer monitoring by an eye care professional, as elevated IOP is a known risk factor for glaucoma. Conversely, an IOP below about 10 mmHg, though less common, can also lead to eye health concerns.
